<!-- PROSE — edit freely in Obsidian -->

A short moral fable in which a peasant named Pahom is offered all the land he can walk around in a single day — but must return to his starting point before sunset or lose everything. His greed drives him to walk further and further, and he dies of exhaustion just as he closes the circle. The answer to the title question: six feet — enough for a grave. [[Leo Tolstoy]] considered this one of his finest short works....
